title: Spiritual Growth
story:
A small child had just learned to drink milk from a cup. Every time he was hungry, he would eagerly reach out for it.

He did not need to be reminded. He did not delay. He knew that milk would satisfy him and help him grow stronger.

As he continued this daily habit, his strength increased. His body developed. What started as a simple need became the foundation of his growth.

In the same way, our spiritual life needs daily nourishment.

Just like a child craves milk, we are invited to desire God's Word. Not out of duty, but with eagerness.

When we spend time with God regularly, something changes within us.

We grow stronger in faith. We become more patient. We begin to see life differently.

Growth does not happen in a single day. But through consistent nourishment, we become who God is shaping us to be.
life_lesson:
Spiritual growth happens when you consistently seek God.

Just as the body needs food, your soul needs God's Word to grow strong and mature.
prayer:
Dear Heavenly Father,

Thank You for giving me Your Word as spiritual nourishment.

Help me to desire it daily, just like a child longs for milk.

Grow my faith and shape my life through Your truth.

Teach me to remain consistent and hungry for Your presence.

Let my life reflect the growth You are working within me.

In Jesus' name,
Amen.
reflection_question:
Do you regularly spend time nourishing your spiritual life?

What is stopping you from seeking God more consistently?

What small step can you take today to grow in your faith?
verse:
Like newborn babies, crave pure spiritual milk.
So that by it you may grow up in your salvation.
Now that you have tasted that the Lord is good.
